title: Macramé placemat set
subtitle: Four simple oval or rectangular placemats knotted in 5 mm natural cotton cord
excerpt: Knot four simple placemats in natural 5 mm cotton cord using alternating square knots on a rectangular warp. Each mat is about 30 x 40 cm and takes one to two hours. The finished set is heat-resistant and wipe-clean.
sourceNotes: Synthesised from contemporary macramé practice. Knotted placemats in natural cotton cord are a current British macramé beginner project; the alternating square knot construction on a horizontal warp is the standard starting format.

glossaryTerms.definition: The core macramé knot: two working cords tied around two filler cords, first left over right, then right over left.

A pattern in which square knots are offset one group to the right or left on each successive row, producing a net-like structure. The filler cords of one knot become the working cords of the adjacent knot on the next row.
The cords that run the full length of a macramé piece, around which the knotting is done. In a placemat, the warp cords run across the shorter dimension.

content.text: on a dowel at the top. Tie
across the mat in offset rows until it reaches the right length. Knot or trim the cord ends at the lower edge.

items.name: Thin wooden dowel, 32 cm long (one per mat)

content.text: Cut 16 lengths of cord, each 120 cm long. Fold each in half and mount on the dowel using lark's head knots, giving 32 working cords. Space the cords about 1 cm apart along the dowel. Pin the dowel to the macramé board.

Tie a square knot with every group of 4 cords across the width. Start from the left: cords 1 and 2 are the working cords, cords 3 and 4 are the filler cords. Tie a complete square knot, then move to the next group of 4 cords.

For row 2, skip the first 2 cords and tie a square knot with cords 3, 4, 5, 6; then cords 7, 8, 9, 10; and so on across the width. The knots sit between the knots of the previous row, creating the net-like pattern. Continue alternating rows until the mat is 40 cm long.

Tie one final row of standard (non-alternating) square knots across all cords to close the edge. Trim the ends to about 2 to 3 cm below the last knot row. Apply a small drop of fabric glue to each trimmed end to prevent fraying, or tie a small overhand knot in each cord tail.

items.problem: The mat narrows significantly toward the bottom
items.cause: The working cords are being pulled inward with each knot
items.fix: Keep the working cords at a consistent angle to the filler cords. Use T-pins at the selvedge to hold the edge cords at the correct width.

items.problem: The knot spacing is uneven across the width
items.cause: The square knots were tied at different tensions
items.fix: Check tension by comparing the knot height across the row before moving to the next. Each square knot should sit at the same distance from the previous row.
